overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active and specialized social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on academic and professional updates in computational social science and disinformation research. With a combined audience of 3,580 followers and 7 recent posts, the profile shows a consistent engagement, with LinkedIn having the largest audience and Bluesky demonstrating the highest average post engagement rate. Content strategy effectively combines research dissemination, career opportunities, and academic discussions.

contentThemes.description: Announcements of new papers, research findings, software releases (e.g., CooRnet v2.0), and grant awards related to disinformation and social media analysis.
Posts related to hiring postdoctoral researchers and PhD student opportunities in computational social science and related fields.
Discussions on research methodologies, upcoming conference presentations, and insights into studying disinformation across platforms.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's higher average engagement rate by using it as a primary platform for immediate announcements of new publications and calls for research-related discussions, potentially exploring more interactive content formats.

Continue to maximize LinkedIn for high-impact professional announcements, such as major grant awards and significant hiring drives, as it consistently garners the highest total interactions and reaches the largest professional audience.

Ensure consistent cross-promotion of academic and career opportunities (postdoc/PhD openings) across all platforms, tailoring the call-to-action to each platform's community norms (e.g., direct replies on Bluesky/Mastodon, formal applications via LinkedIn).

Actively engage with relevant academic hashtags and conference tags (e.g., #DisinfoConf2025) across platforms to increase visibility, foster community engagement, and amplify reach for presentations and discussions.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits the highest average post engagement rate (6.14%), indicating a highly receptive and interactive audience for academic news, especially new publications and conference participation announcements. It is also effective for soliciting interest in research opportunities for students.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon serves a smaller but engaged audience (average 4.55% post engagement rate), primarily interested in detailed research updates (like CooRnet v2.0) and methodological discussions. While engagement volume is lower, the quality of interaction may be higher, suitable for niche academic content.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn boasts the largest follower count (1850) and generates the highest total interactions per post (average 4.87% post engagement rate), making it the most effective platform for professional milestones like grant announcements and significant hiring opportunities. The explicit presence of 'comments' as a metric suggests deeper professional discourse.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
Our team has just released v2.0 of CooRnet, with improved algorithms for detecting coordinated link sharing behavior.
Looking forward to presenting at #DisinfoConf2025 next month on computational methods for detecting coordinated campaigns
